form.title: Specialist verification
form.intro: Register your gut health credentials before joining the NutriMinds specialist community.
details.title: Your details
details.description: We will verify your email before your specialist application enters the review queue.
details.addressHelp: Optional. This helps us match documents when needed.
specialties.title: Choose your profession or specialist field
specialties.description: Select one primary profession and any additional areas relevant to gut, brain, microbiome, nutrition, or adjacent clinical work.
specialties.searchLabel: Search by profession, specialisation, keyword, or category
documents.title: Upload your documents
documents.description: Used only to verify your specialist fields. Backend storage will be protected in the next phase.
documents.identity: ID, driving license, or passport copy
documents.help: PDF, JPG or PNG - max 10 MB
review.title: Review & submit
review.description: Check your information before sending it for verification.
review.terms: I agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy.
review.platformConsent: I consent to my verified specialist profile being created on os.nutriminds.net.
review.prototypeNotice: Frontend prototype complete. Backend submission will be connected in the next phase.
js.noIdentityDocument: No ID, driving license, or passport copy selected
js.submitError: Submission failed. Please check the form and try again.
validation.email: Please enter a valid email address.
validation.phone: Please enter a valid phone number.
validation.fileSize: This file is larger than 10 MB.
validation.fileType: Please upload a PDF, JPG, or PNG file.
validation.specialtiesRequired: Please select at least one profession and choose a primary profession.
validation.termsRequired: Please confirm this consent before submitting.
validation.summaryIntro: We found a few details that need attention before you can continue.
ajax.success: Application received. It is waiting for admin review.
ajax.invalidRequest: The submission could not be verified. Please refresh the page and try again.
ajax.requiredFields: Please complete all required fields and select a primary profession.
ajax.phoneError: Please enter a valid phone number.
ajax.storageError: The application could not be stored. Please try again.
ajax.uploadError: One of the documents could not be uploaded. Please use PDF, JPG, or PNG files up to 10 MB.
email.rejection.subject: Update about your {site} specialist application
email.rejection.body:
Hello {name},

Thank you for your interest in joining {site} as a specialist.

We reviewed the documents you submitted, but we are not able to validate them yet. This does not necessarily mean your professional background is not suitable; it only means the current documents are not enough for our verification step.

You are welcome to review your documents and submit an updated application when you are ready.

Kind regards,
{site}

specialtyGroups.description: Core discipline — diet, mood & mental illness

items.name: Nutritional Psychiatrist
items.description: MD/PhD – prescribes dietary interventions alongside pharmacotherapy for psychiatric conditions

items.name: Clinical Nutritional Psychologist
items.description: PhD – studies the bidirectional relationship between food intake patterns and psychological outcomes

items.name: Research Fellow – Nutritional Psychiatry
items.description: Postdoc or senior researcher in dedicated nutritional psychiatry labs (e.g. Food & Mood Centre)

items.name: Dietary Intervention Trialist
items.description: Designs and runs RCTs (e.g. SMILES-type trials) examining diet-as-medicine in depression/anxiety

items.name: Psychodietary Counsellor
items.description: Advanced-practice clinician integrating psychiatric assessment with personalised dietary therapy

items.name: Nutripsychiatric Nurse Practitioner
items.description: APN with specialist training in nutrition-based mental health protocols

items.name: Eating Behaviour Scientist
items.description: Investigates psychological drivers of food choice and their downstream effect on mood and cognition

items.name: Food-Mood Researcher (MSc/PhD)
items.description: Early-career academic focusing on observational or mechanistic nutritional psychiatry studies

items.name: Psychobiotic Researcher
items.description: Studies pro- and prebiotic effects on neurotransmitter production, HPA axis and mood

items.name: Enteric Neuroscientist
items.description: Specialist in the enteric nervous system and its crosstalk with the CNS

items.name: Clinical Microbiome Specialist
items.description: MD or PhD integrating microbiome diagnostics into patient management

items.name: Gut–Brain Axis Researcher
items.description: Investigates vagal, immune and metabolic pathways linking gut microbiota to brain function

items.name: Microbiome Data Scientist / Bioinformatician
items.description: Analyses 16S rRNA, metagenomics and metabolomics datasets from gut–brain studies

items.name: Gnotobiotic Animal Model Researcher
items.description: Uses germ-free rodent models to study microbiota–behaviour relationships

items.name: Metabolomics Specialist (gut metabolites)
items.description: Profiles short-chain fatty acids, tryptophan metabolites, GABA precursors in biosamples

items.name: Psychobiome Clinician-Researcher
items.description: Translational clinician bridging microbiome lab findings into psychiatric care

items.name: Behavioural Neurologist
items.description: Treats neurological disorders with prominent behavioural/cognitive components linked to systemic inflammation or diet

items.name: Neuropsychiatrist
items.description: Bridges neurology and psychiatry; evaluates gut–brain interactions in conditions like MS, Parkinson's, epilepsy

items.name: Neurogastroenterologist
items.description: MD specialising in the interface of GI and neurological systems (IBS, functional GI disorders, enteric neuropathies)

items.name: Cognitive Neuroscientist
items.description: Studies diet- and microbiome-related effects on cognition, memory and neurodegeneration

items.name: Clinical Neuropsychologist
items.description: Assesses and rehabilitates cognitive dysfunction; interested in nutritional and gut-related contributing factors

items.name: Neurodegenerative Disease Researcher
items.description: Investigates gut–brain links in Alzheimer's, Parkinson's and ALS

items.name: Neuroinflammation Researcher
items.description: Studies how gut-derived cytokines, LPS and SCFAs modulate CNS inflammatory states

items.name: Paediatric Neurologist
items.description: Examines nutrition and microbiome influences on neurodevelopmental conditions (ADHD, ASD, epilepsy)

items.name: Functional Neurologist
items.description: Integrative/functional medicine approach to neurological symptoms including dietary and microbiome factors

items.name: Neuroimmunologist
items.description: Studies CNS–immune system interactions; interested in gut-derived immune signals affecting brain

items.name: Clinical Immunologist
items.description: MD treating immune dysregulation (autoimmunity, food-triggered immune activation) with CNS implications

items.name: Mucosal Immunologist
items.description: Specialist in gut-associated lymphoid tissue (GALT) and IgA/IgG responses at the gut barrier

items.name: Psychoneuroimmunologist
items.description: Studies bidirectional pathways between psychological states, nervous system and immune function

items.name: Inflammasome Researcher
items.description: Investigates NLRP3 and other inflammasome pathways activated by diet-derived signals in psychiatric disease

items.name: Cytokine & Mood Disorder Researcher
items.description: Links pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-α, CRP) to depression, fatigue and cognitive symptoms

items.name: Food Immunologist / Food Allergy Researcher
items.description: Studies immune reactions to food antigens and their systemic effects including neuropsychiatric symptoms

items.name: Autoimmune Encephalitis Specialist
items.description: Clinician-researcher examining gut–immune–brain pathways in autoimmune CNS conditions

items.name: Microbiome Immunologist
items.description: Focuses on how microbial diversity and dysbiosis modulate innate and adaptive immunity

specialtyGroups.description: HPA axis, hormones, metabolic–mental health links

items.name: Psychoneuroendocrinologist
items.description: Studies hormonal regulation of mood, stress and behaviour; HPA axis, cortisol, oxytocin

items.name: Neuroendocrinologist
items.description: Investigates how hypothalamic–pituitary hormonal axes interface with gut-derived signals

items.name: Metabolic Psychiatrist
items.description: Addresses metabolic syndrome, insulin resistance and adipokines as risk factors in psychiatric disorders

items.name: Clinical Endocrinologist
items.description: MD treating thyroid, adrenal and metabolic disorders with mental health manifestations

items.name: Gut Hormone Researcher
items.description: Studies GLP-1, PYY, ghrelin, CCK and other enteroendocrine signals affecting appetite, mood and cognition

items.name: Stress Biology Researcher
items.description: Examines cortisol, allostatic load and HPA reactivity in relation to gut permeability and mental health

items.name: Chrono-nutritionist / Circadian Biologist
items.description: Studies time-of-eating, circadian gene expression and their effects on mood, metabolism and microbiome

items.name: Insulin Resistance & Brain Health Researcher
items.description: Links type-2 diabetes pathways, brain insulin signalling and dementia/depression risk

items.name: Adipokine & Neuroinflammation Researcher
items.description: Studies leptin, adiponectin and obesity-driven inflammation in psychiatric disease

items.name: Medical Psychologist
items.description: PhD/MD-PhD applying psychological models within medical settings; focuses on chronic disease, pain and gut symptoms

items.name: Psychosomatic Physician
items.description: MD integrating somatic and psychological diagnosis; frequently encounters functional GI disorders

items.name: Health Psychologist (chronic disease)
items.description: Studies behavioural, cognitive and emotional factors in IBD, IBS, food intolerances and immune disorders

items.name: Somatic Symptom Disorder Specialist
items.description: Clinician-researcher focusing on medically unexplained symptoms with gut–brain underpinnings

items.name: Biofeedback & Neurofeedback Clinician
items.description: Uses autonomic and brainwave feedback to modulate stress, gut motility and emotional regulation

items.name: Stress & Resilience Researcher
items.description: Examines psychological resilience, adverse childhood experiences and their gut-microbiome signatures

items.name: Interoception Researcher
items.description: Studies gut-to-brain interceptive signalling and its role in emotional awareness and mental health

items.name: Pain Psychologist
items.description: Addresses visceral hypersensitivity, central sensitisation and chronic pain with gut–brain perspectives

items.name: Mind–Body Medicine Researcher
items.description: Investigates meditation, relaxation and lifestyle interventions on gut function and psychiatric endpoints

items.name: Liaison Psychiatrist
items.description: Works at the interface of physical and mental illness in hospital settings; frequently addresses GI comorbidities

specialtyGroups.description: From bench to bedside — omics, biomarkers, clinical trials

items.name: Translational Neuroscientist
items.description: Bridges preclinical gut–brain findings to clinical application and trial design

items.name: Precision Nutrition Researcher
items.description: Uses genomics, microbiomics and metabolomics to personalise dietary recommendations

items.name: Biomarker Discovery Scientist
items.description: Identifies blood, stool or CSF biomarkers linking nutritional status to psychiatric phenotypes

items.name: Clinical Trial Designer (psychiatric nutrition)
items.description: Specialist in RCT methodology for dietary and probiotic interventions in mental health populations

items.name: Epigenetics Researcher (diet & brain)
items.description: Studies how diet-induced epigenetic changes (DNA methylation, histone modification) affect psychiatric risk

items.name: Multi-omics Data Integrator
items.description: Combines transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics and microbiomics for gut–brain phenotyping

items.name: Pharmacomicrobiomics Researcher
items.description: Investigates how gut microbiota modulates drug metabolism, efficacy and psychiatric side-effects

items.name: Systems Biology Scientist
items.description: Models complex gut–brain–immune networks using computational approaches

items.name: Professor of Nutritional Psychiatry / Neuroscience
items.description: Chair-level academic leading research groups in the field

items.name: Associate / Assistant Professor
items.description: Early–mid career faculty in nutritional psychiatry, neuroscience or related disciplines

items.name: Principal Investigator (PI)
items.description: Grant-holding researcher running independent lab or clinical research unit

items.name: Postdoctoral Research Fellow
items.description: Post-PhD researcher with specialisation in gut–brain, nutrition–mood or neuroimmunology

items.name: MD-PhD Clinician-Scientist
items.description: Dual-trained physician-researcher at the translational interface of psychiatry and nutritional science

items.name: ECNP / CINP Member (Neuropsychopharmacology)
items.description: Member of European/International Collegium of Neuropsychopharmacology with nutrition focus

items.name: ISAD Member (Int'l Society for Affective Disorders)
items.description: Researcher or clinician with interest in nutritional and biological factors in mood disorders

items.name: ISMB / ISME Member (Microbiome Research)
items.description: Affiliated with international microbiome research societies

items.name: Fellow of the Royal College of Psychiatrists (FRCPsych)
items.description: Senior clinical-academic psychiatrist in the UK/Commonwealth system

items.name: FASN / FSNI (Neuroscience Institute Fellow)
items.description: Fellow of national or international neuroscience institutes
